name: Pie Shell for Pecan Pie
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons chilled unsalted butter, cut into 1/4-inch pats
2 tablespoons vegetable shortening, frozen solid and cut into small pieces
1 large egg white, thoroughly mixed with ice water
1 large egg yolk, beaten with 1/8 teaspoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Scatter butter and shortening over dry ingredients and pulse until mixture resembles coarse cornmeal (10-15 seconds). Transfer mixture to a medium bowl.
2. Sprinkle egg white mixture over the flour mixture and, using a folding motion with a rubber spatula, mix until dough forms a ball. Shape dough into a 4-inch disk, dust lightly with flour, wrap tightly in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days.
3. Roll dough on a lightly floured surface into a 13-inch circle and transfer to a 9-inch pie pan, preferably glass. Press dough into corners and sides of the pan, being careful not to stretch it. Trim edges of dough to create a 1/2-inch overhang. Tuck overhanging dough under so that the folded edge is flush with the rim of the pan. Flute the edge.
4. Chill the shell until firm (about 1 hour). Prick the sides and bottom with a fork and line the entire shell with heavy-duty aluminum foil, pressing firmly against the shell and extending it over the fluted rim. Prick the foil with a fork and return the shell to the refrigerator while the oven is heating.
5. Adjust the oven rack to the center position and preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Bake, pressing gently if necessary to flatten any puffing, until the crust is firmly set (about 15 minutes). Remove the foil and continue baking until the bottom begins to color (about 10 minutes longer). Remove from the oven, brush the sides and bottom with egg yolk, and return to the oven until the yolk glazes over (about 1 minute longer).
